macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto market sees nearly $658M in liquidations as Bitcoin and Ether slide
items.summary: The crypto market was hit by a major sell-off over the past 24 hours, with total liquidations approaching $658 million. Ethereum led losses with about $256.8 million in long liquidations, while Bitcoin accounted for roughly $180.9 million as BTC fell below $77,000 and ETH dropped under $2,120.

items.title: Crypto investment products post $1.07B in outflows amid Iran tensions
items.summary: Cryptocurrency investment products recorded significant outflows as investors reduced risk exposure during heightened geopolitical tensions. The report notes Bitcoin traded around $77,381 while major altcoins such as Ethereum, BNB, and XRP also declined.

items.title: Market-wide crypto pullback drags Bitcoin to a two-week low
items.summary: A broader crypto sell-off pushed the overall market down about 2% in 24 hours, with Bitcoin leading the decline. The move was accompanied by a fresh wave of liquidations across digital assets.

items.title: Crypto news roundup highlights ETF weakness, treasury buying, and bridge exploit
items.summary: Recent headlines include Ethereum treasury accumulation by Bitmine, Goldman Sachs trimming exposure to XRP and Solana ETFs, and a Verus-Ethereum bridge exploit that reportedly drained $11.58 million. These developments point to continued volatility alongside active institutional and DeFi news flow.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Boston Dynamics says Atlas is learning whole-body manipulation for real-world work
items.summary: Boston Dynamics released a behind-the-scenes update on Atlas, saying the humanoid robot is being trained with reinforcement learning and control systems to handle heavy objects, balance, and navigate dynamic industrial tasks. The company frames the work as a step toward humanoids operating in real-world settings beyond the lab.

items.title: AI show highlights a new 1-trillion-parameter reasoning model claiming strong benchmark performance
items.summary: A recent AI news roundup discussed a new reasoning model described as having 1 trillion parameters and an 'extra high' thinking mode. The segment claimed the model outperformed some leading systems on benchmarks, though the presentation was informal and should be treated as a product demonstration rather than an independent evaluation.

items.title: Robotics demo pits a humanoid system against a human in warehouse-style sorting
items.summary: A livestream covered a long-running human-versus-robot warehouse sorting challenge, with the robot and a human intern competing to scan barcodes and sort boxes. The event was presented as a showcase of humanoid automation progress and the pace of improvement in logistics robotics.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: OpenAI jury rules against Elon Musk in high-profile case
items.summary: A jury has ruled against Elon Musk after a three-week trial, ending with less than two hours of deliberation. The decision is one of the biggest recent tech-industry legal developments.

items.title: Sony raises PS Plus prices for one- and three-month subscriptions
items.summary: Sony is increasing PlayStation Plus prices for shorter subscription terms, citing ongoing market conditions. The move will affect users who prefer monthly or quarterly plans.

items.title: Microsoft ends Teams 'Together mode' feature
items.summary: Microsoft says it is ditching the Teams feature that placed attendees into the same virtual room, citing too much implementation complexity. The company says it will steer users toward the standard gallery view instead.

items.title: Tesla robotaxi crash data highlights slow progress in rollout
items.summary: New crash data points to limited but continuing safety issues in Tesla's robotaxi testing program, including incidents involving remote operators. The report suggests progress in the autonomous ride-hailing rollout has been slower than expected.

items.title: Bill Gates-backed TerraPower acquires South Korean tech for US sodium-cooled reactor
items.summary: TerraPower has acquired South Korean technology needed for a 345-MW sodium-cooled reactor project in the US. The transfer also enables the company to build and operate its own safety testing facility.

items.title: ITER tokamak begins commissioning after major coil upgrade
items.summary: The world’s largest operating tokamak has entered commissioning with an upgraded set of 8-meter coils. Scientists from EUROfusion and ITER will gather data that could help inform future fusion reactors.
